name: Cindy McCain
summary: Widow of Senator John McCain who endorsed Joe Biden on September 22, 2020, citing a country-first ethic and calling Biden a good and honest man.

keyStatements.quote: My husband John lived by a code: country first. We are Republicans, yes, but Americans foremost. There's only one candidate in this race who stands up for our values as a nation, and that is Joe Biden.
Joe and I don't always agree, but he is a good and honest man and I believe he has the power to bring this country together.
There is too much at risk in this election to sit on the sidelines.

timeline.event: Witnessed Trump's attacks on her husband
Biden announced intent to nominate her as UN food agencies ambassador
timeline.significance: Selected for U.S. Ambassador to UN Agencies for Food and Agriculture

credibility.background: Widow of Senator John McCain, businesswoman, humanitarian, U.S. Ambassador
positions: U.S. Ambassador to UN Agencies for Food and Agriculture (2021-present)

oppositionReasons: Cited a country-first ethic in opposing Trump
Praised Biden as a good and honest man who could unite the country
Said the stakes were too high to sit out the election

sources.title: Press Release: Cindy McCain Endorses Joe Biden for President
Biden will nominate Cindy McCain as ambassador to UN food agencies
